name: Juliana Stratton
bio: Juliana Stratton is the Lieutenant Governor of Illinois, serving alongside Gov. J.B. Pritzker since 2019. She previously served in the Illinois General Assembly and is running for the open U.S. Senate seat with Pritzker's backing. (Source: FEC S6IL00458; WTTW Voter Guide 2026)
prior_office: Lieutenant Governor of Illinois (2019–present); Illinois State Representative

jacket.note: Approximately $4M raised directly to campaign committee (FEC S6IL00458). Separately, Gov. Pritzker donated $5M to an independent expenditure PAC supporting Stratton — this is NOT campaign committee money and is tracked in pac_support_total. ≥27 AIPAC-network donors gave $70K+ directly to campaign committee (The Intercept, March 12, 2026).

donors.name: Gov. J.B. Pritzker (via independent expenditure PAC — NOT campaign committee funds)
donors.label: Independent Expenditure / PAC — NOT campaign committee funds

donors.name: AIPAC-network donors (≥27 individuals — direct to campaign committee)
donors.label: Direct contributions to campaign committee

red_flags.label: AIPAC Donor Network — $70K+ Direct + Former AIPAC President on Finance Committee
red_flags.detail: The Intercept reported on March 12, 2026 that at least 27 AIPAC donors gave $70K+ directly to Stratton's Senate campaign. Lee Rosenberg, a former president of AIPAC, sits on her campaign finance committee. AIPAC is officially staying neutral in the IL Senate race but its donor network is active for Stratton.

endorsements.significance: $5M PAC support; governor's endorsement carries major downstate name recognition

career_history.highlight: Running mate to Gov. JB Pritzker; broke barriers as first Black Lt. Governor
House Judiciary Committee; criminal justice reform focus

career_history.org: University of Illinois Chicago School of Law
career_history.highlight: Adjunct professor; focused on legal aid and social justice

policy_platform.position: Championed sentencing reform, rehabilitation over punishment, and reducing mass incarceration in Illinois.
Supports equitable school funding, early childhood education investment.
Supported Illinois cannabis legalization (2019); advocate for equity in licensing and expungement of prior convictions.

social_pulse.summary: Positive sentiment among progressives and African American communities. Moderate statewide name recognition. Benefits from incumbency in Lt. Governor role.

trust_indicators.label: Consistent progressive voting record in General Assembly
First Black Lt. Governor of Illinois
